items.title: 'Under siege': Uganda's army chief clamps down on the press

items.title: 'One last crack' to restart Whyalla’s blast furnace as workers face nervous wait

items.title: 'Targeting of Palestinian children has not stopped': Independent UN investigator sounds alarm
items.description: Independent UN investigators are calling for action after releasing a report detailing what they call “deliberate killing of Palestinian children” by Israel. The Independent International Commission of Inquiry says that since October 7, 2023, the "essence of childhood" has been "destroyed" in Gaza and the West Bank. Speaking with FRANCE 24, Commission Chair Srinivasan Muralidhar said that among the vast evidence collected were testimonies from doctors who said children were admitted to their care with "gunshot wounds specifically targeted at the head and the neck to cause maximum damage".

items.title: 'Politically expedient around the world': Immigrants in South Africa face wrath of xenophobia
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Aimée-Noel Mbiyozo, Senior Research Consultant at the Institute for Security Studies (ISS). South Africa's recurring waves of anti migrant mobilisation reveal a deeper political and socioeconomic paradox. Mbiyozo argues that while public frustration over unemployment, inequality, corruption and failing public services is genuine, migrants have become convenient political scapegoats rather than the cause of these structural crises. Drawing on migration data, governance failures and labour market realities, she contends that anti migrant narratives persist not because they are supported by evidence, but because they offer politically expedient explanations for complex problems.

items.description: A loss of democratic credibility would undercut diplomatic gains and strengthen M23’s demands for autonomy in eastern DRC. The post Does Tshisekedi’s third-term bid benefit DRCongo? appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Oil set for steepest quarterly loss since 2020 as traders focus on US-Iran talks
Usman Khawaja helps launch national Islamophobia awareness campaign
items.description: The retired 39-year-old, who was the first Muslim to play international cricket for Australia, says Islamophobia is on the rise, with women most vulnerable to attacks, because "hate is more prevalent that it has been before".

items.title: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country
items.description: An opposition leader in the Democratic Republic of Congo said Monday he had been banned from leaving the country and stripped of his passport, condemning it as "intimidation" amid tensions with the government.

items.title: 🔴 US Supreme Court upholds transgender sports bans
Flooding hits Ghana's capital killing 13 people
items.description: People have been urged to relocate to high ground or stay indoors as more rain is expected to come.

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Peru: Fujimori declared winner of presidential election
items.description: After weeks of reviewing the ballots, Peru’s electoral commission declared Keiko Fujimori the winner of the presidential election.

items.title: More than one million migrants apply for legal status in Spain
items.description: More than one million undocumented migrants and asylum seekers have applied for legal residency under Spain's mass regularisation programme, marking a significant response to one of Europe's largest immigration initiatives.

items.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
items.description: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

items.title: Kenya's iconic flamingos lose their pink as rift valley lakes change
items.description: Conservationists warn that protecting these fragile ecosystems will be critical to ensuring future generations can continue to witness the spectacular pink flocks that have long defined Kenya's Great Rift Valley.

items.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
items.description: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.title: Venezuelans deported from the U.S. were killed hours later in powerful quakes
items.description: On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.

items.title: Buenos Aires City poverty rate rises after five quarters of decline
items.description: Over 651,000 porteños – more than one in five residents – were living in poverty in the first quarter of 2026, according to the IDECBA city statistics bureau. Leer más

items.title: Putin makes rare admission of fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian strikes
items.description: The Russian president acknowledged Ukraine's attacks were "obviously creating problems" but denied the shortages were "critical".

items.title: Venezuela appreciates Indian team’s efforts to help earthquake victims
items.description: The death toll from two powerful quakes in Venezuela crossed 1,700 on June 29, with thousands more injured and many more missing

items.title: Senegal MPs back sweeping constitutional reform
items.description: Police fired tear gas as protesters clashed with security outside Senegal's National Assembly, where lawmakers approved a major constitutional reform that would significantly reduce presidential powers.

items.title: Milei looks to Santilli to move beyond Adorni chapter
items.description: President hopes trusted former PRO operator can help La Libertad Avanza regain the initiative after Manuel Adorni's resignation and push the scandal off the agenda. Leer más

items.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave
items.description: A record-breaking heat wave has moved on, with temperatures sinking rapidly amid many storms. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is heading to Washington for talks with his US counterpart, with NATO issues in focus.

items.title: The revolving door at 10 Downing Street
Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
items.description: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

items.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
items.description: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

items.title: Press freedom under threat in Uganda as military chief orders closures
items.description: Independent media group, NMG, said it was under a "military siege" after Muhoozi Kainerugaba shutdown its newspapers, TV station, and radio outlets.

items.title: Private channel suspended in Pakistan for 15 days over sectarian content
items.description: The Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority on June 29 suspended with “immediate effect” Geo News’ licence for 15 days, saying the channel was “liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers”

items.title: US top court backs birthright citizenship in rebuke to Trump
items.description: US President Donald Trump had issued an executive order that would have changed constitutional guarantees that people born on US soil are citizens.

items.title: Migrant workers struggle to survive as Southern Italy bakes
items.description: Under Italy's scorching heatwave, thousands of migrant workers are struggling to survive in a sprawling shanty town outside Foggia.

items.title: Milei’s Cabinet chief resigns months after private jet scandal
items.description: Resignation marks a sharp reversal for the government after President Milei himself repeatedly brushed off the revelations about Manuel Adorni, deriding them as attacks orchestrated by the opposition and local media. Leer más

items.title: US Supreme Court hands Trump 3-1 defeat in key rulings: What we know
items.description: The justices deliver consequential rulings on mail-in ballots, the E Jean Carroll case and the firing of Lisa Cook.

items.title: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu
items.description: The European Union is imposing new restrictions on visas for Somali nationals after a disagreement with Mogadishu over the return of irregular migrants to their home country.

items.title: Joby, Toyota announce joint venture for air taxi production
Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
items.description: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

items.title: France vs Sweden: World Cup Round of 32 – Mbappe, prediction, team lineups
items.description: Kylian Mbappe's France, the title favourites for the 2026 World Cup, take on Alexander Isak's Sweden in the last 32.

items.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
items.description: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

items.title: Experts: Hantavirus detected in Tierra del Fuego, but no link to cruise outbreak
items.description: Experts detect hantavirus in rodents captured in Tierra del Fuego Province, although the strain differs from the one responsible for the outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. Leer más

items.title: Poland recognises same-sex marriages, sees rush for weddings organised abroad
items.description: Poland is one of the most restrictive countries in Europe for LGBT rights. Neither marriages nor civil partnerships for same-sex couples are legal there. But as of late May, Poland has legalised the recognition of same-sex marriages performed in third countries. The change comes after the European Court of Justice ruled that Warsaw was in contravention of EU free movement laws if it withdrew rights that couples had acquired in another country. As a result, Polish same-sex couples have been rushing to organise weddings outside the country. FRANCE 24's Adrien Sarlat reports, with Gulliver Cragg.

items.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire despite ceasefire. And, Trump nominates a new head of ICE
items.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

items.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
items.description: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

items.title: Chinese tech companies seek nearly $6 billion in Hong Kong listing rush
Supreme Court orders Milei government to fund universities
items.description: Ruling upholds university financing law vetoed by the President and ends a two-year legal battle over funding, salaries and student support. Leer más

items.description: WhatsApp users will soon have the option to use usernames instead of phone numbers

items.title: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
items.description: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

items.description: As Nigeria moves closer to the 2027 general elections, it is important to remind all the actors involved in this process to act in a responsible manner and not engage in any untidy or sharp practice that may raise doubts or anxiety about the integrity of the elections. items.title: Germany's development aid strategy under fire
items.description: Days ahead of the launch of the North-South Commission, development aid organizations have urged the German government to reverse billions in cuts to development aid.

items.title: Japan says ready to act as yen hits 40-year low
items.description: The yen sank past 161.96 per dollar in London trade on Monday (June 29, 2026) for the first time since 1986

items.title: Reasonable to believe targeted killing of children part of larger plan for genocide of the Palestinian people, Justice Muralidhar says
items.description: Once a child is labelled a terrorist, he becomes ‘free game’, ‘target practice’

items.title: Biden blasts Trump 'vanity projects', calling him a 'loser' at fundraising event
items.description: The former US president took aim at his successor's projects in Washington DC, including a planned new White House ballroom.

items.title: The next British PM should steer clear of Starmer’s shameful legacy
items.description: The outgoing PM succeeded in enabling genocide and failed miserably at improving the lives of ordinary Brits.

items.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
items.description: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

items.title: Caracas park helps children cope through activities after twin quakes
items.description: A public park in Caracas has been transformed into a hub of activities for children to help them cope after twin quakes.

items.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
items.description: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

items.title: Japan keeps intervention rhetoric unchanged despite yen's slide to 40-year low
South African police general shot days before corruption inquiry testimony
items.description: A senior South African police official has been hospitalised after gunmen opened fire on his vehicle in Johannesburg, days before he was expected to appear before a high-profile inquiry into alleged corruption and criminal networks within the country's police service.

items.title: 'No place for the poison of antisemitism': New warning for universities
items.description: The government's Antisemitism Education Taskforce, led by David Gonski, has equipped teachers with new resources to tackle antisemitism and promote "Australian values".
